numeral.purpose: Playing digits in the grid and number pad; weight stays 400 at all sizes.

body.purpose: Stats row, in-card messages, secondary actions.

motion.name: press-feedback
motion.purpose: The only motion. Touch targets dim on press; no custom transitions, no choreography.

motion.name: reduced-motion
motion.purpose: Calm-by-default extends to motion; honor prefers-reduced-motion with instant state changes.

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: Solid-fill primary action: 'Continue game' on home, 'New game' in the win/lose card.
components.css: .ds-btn-primary { background: #2F6BFF; color: #FFFFFF; font: 700 17px/1.2 -apple-system, system-ui, 'Segoe UI', Roboto, sans-serif; padding: 16px 28px; border: none; border-radius: 14px; cursor: pointer; transition: opacity 0.15s ease-out; } .ds-btn-primary:active { opacity: 0.7; } .ds-btn-primary:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #2F6BFF; outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Difficulty List Button
components.description: Surface row with a label and chevron; the standard 'tap to go' affordance on the home screen.
components.css: .ds-list-button { display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: space-between; width: 280px; background: #FFFFFF; color: #1A1A1A; font: 600 18px/1.3 -apple-system, system-ui, 'Segoe UI', Roboto, sans-serif; padding: 18px 20px; border: 1px solid #D3D6DE; border-radius: 14px; cursor: pointer; transition: opacity 0.15s ease-out; } .ds-list-button:active { opacity: 0.7; } .ds-list-button:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #2F6BFF; outline-offset: 2px; } .ds-chevron { color: #686D78; font-size: 22px; }

components.name: Number Pad Digit
components.description: Text-only digit with a remaining count beneath. Active (fast-mode selected) turns Focus Blue; exhausted fades.
components.css: .ds-numpad { display: flex; gap: 24px; background: #F2F3F7; padding: 12px; } .ds-digit { display: flex; flex-direction: column; align-items: center; background: none; border: none; color: #1A1A1A; font: 400 34px/1 -apple-system, system-ui, 'Segoe UI', Roboto, sans-serif; cursor: pointer; } .ds-digit--active { color: #2F6BFF; } .ds-digit--done { color: #686D78; opacity: 0.35; cursor: default; } .ds-count { color: #686D78; font-size: 12px; margin-top: -2px; }

components.name: Sudoku Cell (states)
components.description: The signature component. Default surface cell, peer-highlighted, same-value tinted, selected (accent fill), and conflict (error red).
components.html:
5
3
7
7
7
1
components.css: .ds-grid { display: flex; border: 2px solid #6B7280; width: max-content; } .ds-cell { width: 44px; height: 44px; display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; background: #FFFFFF; color: #2F6BFF; font: 400 26px/1 -apple-system, system-ui, 'Segoe UI', Roboto, sans-serif; border-right: 1px solid #D3D6DE; } .ds-cell--given { color: #1A1A1A; } .ds-cell--peer { background: #E8EBF2; } .ds-cell--same { background: #D5DEF7; } .ds-cell--selected { background: #2F6BFF; color: #FFFFFF; } .ds-cell--conflict { color: #E5484D; }

components.name: Tool Button
components.description: Icon-over-label control (Undo / Erase / Fast Pencil / Pencil). Active state is Focus Blue with an ON/OFF pill badge; disabled is muted.
components.css: .ds-tools { display: flex; gap: 16px; } .ds-tool { display: flex; flex-direction: column; align-items: center; gap: 4px; min-width: 64px; background: none; border: none; color: #1A1A1A; font: 400 13px/1.3 -apple-system, system-ui, 'Segoe UI', Roboto, sans-serif; cursor: pointer; } .ds-tool-icon { position: relative; font-size: 24px; } .ds-tool--active { color: #2F6BFF; } .ds-tool--disabled { color: #686D78; cursor: default; } .ds-badge { position: absolute; top: -6px; right: -16px; background: #2F6BFF; color: #FFFFFF; font-size: 9px; font-weight: 700; border-radius: 8px; padding: 1px 5px; }

components.name: Theme Swatch
components.description: Palette selector tile showing the theme's canvas, accent dot, and name. Selected gets a 3px accent border.
components.html:
Classic Light
Forest
components.css: .ds-swatches { display: flex; gap: 10px; } .ds-swatch { display: flex; flex-direction: column; justify-content: space-between; width: 96px; height: 64px; background: #F2F3F7; border: 1px solid #D3D6DE; border-radius: 12px; padding: 8px; cursor: pointer; } .ds-swatch--selected { border: 3px solid #2F6BFF; } .ds-swatch-dot { width: 16px; height: 16px; border-radius: 8px; background: #2F6BFF; } .ds-swatch-name { font: 600 12px/1.2 -apple-system, system-ui, 'Segoe UI', Roboto, sans-serif; color: #1A1A1A; } .ds-swatch--forest { background: #F1F5F0; } .ds-swatch--forest .ds-swatch-dot { background: #2E7D4F; }

narrative.overview: A Sudoku game that behaves like a still, orderly surface you think on — paper-calm, never clamoring. The whole interface is built around a single act of restraint: the 9×9 grid is the hero of every screen, and everything else recedes to the edges until needed. The touchstone is NYT Games — generous whitespace, confident typography, state communicated through quiet tints rather than chrome. Depth is tonal layering and hairline rules, never shadows. It rejects the ad-cluttered free-puzzle-app look and the loud/gamified look, while refusing to curdle into sterile spreadsheet-grey. Color is a signal, not an ornament: one accent per palette marks selection, active digit, and current tool, and appears nowhere else. Four palettes share one role structure; design against the roles, never a hardcoded hex.
keyCharacteristics: The grid is the hero; every other element earns its place or steps aside.
Flat by default — tonal layering and hairlines, zero decorative shadow.
One accent per palette, used only for state (selection / active / focus).
System font throughout; hierarchy comes from weight and size, not families.
Four palettes, one role structure — design against roles, never hex.

rules.name: The One Signal Rule
rules.body: Each palette has exactly one accent, reserved for state — selection, active digit, active tool, primary action. Emphasis comes from weight, size, and space, never from using the accent as decoration.

rules.name: The Error-Only Red Rule
rules.body: Red is forbidden anywhere except a genuine conflict or mistake. No red badges, dividers, or 'hot' styling. When a player sees red, it means exactly one thing.

rules.name: The Light-Numeral Rule
rules.body: Playing digits stay at weight 400 at any size. Given clues and player entries are distinguished by color (ink vs. accent), never by bolding.

rules.name: The One-Family Rule
rules.body: No second typeface, ever. More hierarchy is achieved with weight and size, not a new font.

rules.name: The Flat-By-Tone Rule
rules.body: Never add box-shadow or elevation to lift an element. Separate it with surface-over-canvas tone or a hairline border. If tone isn't enough, the layout is too crowded.

rules.name: The Hairline Rule
rules.body: Borders are 1px (grid-line) by default. The only heavier stroke is the 2px grid-line-bold marking 3×3 box boundaries and the board frame — structural, never decorative.

dos: Do treat the grid as the hero — most space and contrast on any screen; everything else recedes.
Do design against color roles (surface, ink, primary), never a hardcoded hex; every screen must read in all four palettes including dark Midnight.
Do convey depth with tone and hairlines: surface over app-canvas, 1px grid-line borders, 2px grid-line-bold for box structure.
Do reserve the accent for state — selection, active digit, active tool, primary action — and nothing else.
Do distinguish given clues from player entries by color (ink vs. accent), keeping both numerals at weight 400.
Do pair every error signal with more than color — position, an icon, or a shape — so conflicts are legible to color-blind players (WCAG AA floor).
Do keep body and digit text at AA contrast (≥4.5:1; ≥3:1 large/bold); reserve ink-muted strictly for non-essential secondary labels.
donts: Don't add box-shadow, glassmorphism, or any 'floating card' elevation. This system is flat by tone.
Don't use the accent — or any saturated color — as decoration; use weight, size, or space instead.
Don't use red for anything but a genuine conflict or mistake. No red badges, dividers, or 'hot' styling.
Don't introduce a second typeface or bold the playing numerals for emphasis.
Don't drift toward the ad-cluttered free-puzzle-app look: no banners, coin/streak bait, interstitials, popups, or nag screens.
Don't drift toward the loud/gamified look: no confetti on routine actions, badges, cartoon mascots, or saturated full-bleed color.
Don't over-correct into sterile spreadsheet-grey — calm is not joyless; warmth comes from spacing, airy numerals, and the palette.
Don't use side-stripe accent borders (border-left/border-right > 1px as a colored stripe) on cards or list rows.
